subject: Ecology
title: The Wood Wide Web
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Wood Wide Web.

hook.text: Trees in a forest share nutrients through underground fungal networks.

buildup.text: Mycorrhizal fungi connect roots of different trees, trading minerals for sugars.

discovery.text: Mother trees send carbon to shaded seedlings through these networks to help them grow.

twist.text: Some trees even warn neighbors of insect attacks through chemical signals in the web.

climax.text: Clear-cutting destroys these networks, leaving replanted seedlings isolated and weaker.

punchline.text: Trees talk underground. We just started listening.

quiz.question: What do mycorrhizal fungi do for trees?
options: Connect roots and trade minerals for sugars
